calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-24-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Oct-21-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. AZIN JALALI, Esq. of Pillsbury & Coleman, LLP (Tel: 415-433-8000) for plaintiff Lakshmi Paladugu;
Atty. JEFFREY W. AMLOYAN, Esq. of O'Hagan Meyer LLP (Tel: 310-807-1100) for defendant Bruce Yu Chen;
Atty. ADELLE GREENFIELD, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Combined Insurance Company of America.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BRUCE CHEN'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Bruce Yu Chen's "motion for transfer of venue" is denied.

The complaint pleads that Chen sold plaintiff a Combined Insurance Company of America life insurance policy with a long-term-care rider. A coverage dispute arose. Combined is a foreign corporation with no principal place of business in California, so it can be sued in any California county. (Jalali Dec. Ex. 1; Easton v. Sup. Ct. (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246-47.) Moreover, as Chen states, "so long as the plaintiff chooses a venue that is proper as to one defendant [here San Francisco], the entire case may be tried there." (Memo. 4:12-13, citing Monogram Co. v. Kingsley (1951) 38 Cal.2d 28, 29.)

Chen moves to transfer under CCP 397 "on the ground that" Los Angeles County "is the proper venue." (Memo. 1:21-27.) However, as shown above, San Francisco is a proper venue as well; no one "the proper venue" exists for this case. (See Easton, 12 Cal.App.3d at 247 [movant's burden "of showing the action was not begun in a proper county"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
